content:
@everyone I'd like to start this channel as a hub for ideas on how we can all collaborate together better.
If anyone has any ideas on systemic ways we can make things easier for everyone, or even just complaints about problems that could be solved, please drop them here!

||*~~I need personnel for ID team~~*||

I would ask for an idea to simplify the translation. xD
the list I took from sources is jumbled up..

My initial thought was to have a separate repo that I manage, so that files could be uploaded and merged there before getting uploaded to the main BIAV repo, to make the process simpler for folks unfamiliar with any step of that process and lighten the burden on Ace from having to do that compilation work.

Trying to figure out how best to implement that, and would like some opinions on what that could or should look like, and what problems it needs to solve to be worthwhile.

what do you mean by this exactly? like, organize the main source sheets' rows? trying to do that as best I can but got sidetracked with this repo thought.
I definitely think the sheets should be cut into much more approachable chunks
Yes.. it gets a bit confusing to find which is where, and as I worked some, I found out that there are some lines that mixed up one another
in which category? I know Gameplay is effectively shuffled, but when I looked at Story it seemed relatively put-together (but I didn't look much)
okay, I'll see what I can do. thanks!
for gameplay, I don't know waht you do, but the last excel you sent which have WoD content is very helpful as it significantly increased my working speed

# My initial thoughts for this:
I'd like to have a public repo any of us can push to. I'd have the source sheets cut up into manageable chunks and separated into their own folders, and each folder would have a text file that lists who's working on that chunk in which language;

when someone picks up a task, they download it and sign their name on that file. that way we wouldn't have multiple people working on the same files in the same language at the same time, contribution credits will be more clear, and it'll be easier to know what does and doesn't need work done.

when they're done, they would upload JUST the "Key" column and the column for their language, and I would confirm and commit that into the base file, for the next person to download and work on; and when Ace is ready to upload our work to BIAV, I can wrap everything that's been changed into a single organized file and save Ace all that work.

Thoughts?
Having the source sheet cut up into smth more manageable would feel so much better imo
Trying to locate something specific does feel incredibly daunting, especially if it's something large like a special ops or side story
I think it'd also help visualise our workloads better and organise who takes what instead of just a shot in the dark and picking whatever
not me just doing texts because it's "organized"
haha okay well if my initial concept sounds good I'll go ahead and start with that lol

Agree to the idea of a public repo, thinking that should make working progress much clearer 
And slight modifications on existing files might be easier and don’t have to bother Ace over and over again under this repo imo
However since cutting the source sheet may resulting in a whole bunch of files and might seems confusing, maybe it will be fine if starting from the parts that we mainly working on recently
